plan_metadata.name: Feature DA No Action Add ApiKey Auth Configurations

steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" modal of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No Action" option from the dropdown menu under "Add an action to your declarative agent" in the "Create Declarative Agent" window of Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit.
Click the "Start with a New API" option in the "Create an Action" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the input field labeled "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location or Open File..." in the OpenAPI Spec Document dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Click the text input field labeled "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location" in the OpenAPI Description Document prompt within Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL" input field in the OpenAPI Description Document dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Type the OpenAPI specification URL into the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL" input field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter to confirm the URL input in the "OpenAPI Description Document" address bar in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "OK" button in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" dialog at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the blue "Clear All" button in the dropdown for operation selection within the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension in Visual Studio Code, to deselect all chosen APIs.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select '/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects' as the project location in Visual Studio Code.
Press the F1 key in the Visual Studio Code application to open the Command Palette from the main Explorer view.
Type 'Add configurations to Support' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top of the interface.
Press Enter to select "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Configurations to Support Actions with Authentication" from the VS Code command palette.
Press Enter to confirm the selection of "ai-plugin.json" in the "Import Manifest File" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Type 'apiKey' into the input field of the "Enter the Name of Auth Configuration" dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Press Enter to confirm the input "apiKey" in the "Enter the Name of Auth Configuration" prompt in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Select an authentication type" input field in the authentication dropdown dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Type 'API Key' into the search field of the 'Authentication Type' dropdown prompt in Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter to select "API Key" from the Authentication Type dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter to select the "Header" option in the "Enter where the API Key should be in the request" dropdown prompt in Visual Studio Code.
Type 'X-API-KEY' into the input field of the "Enter the Name of API Key" prompt in Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter in the "Enter the Name of API Key" input box in Visual Studio Code to confirm the entry "apiKey".
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in the Visual Studio Code Explorer view.
Type 'Notifications: Show Notifications' into the VS Code Command Palette input box at the top of the window.
Press Enter to select "Notifications: Show Notifications" from the command palette dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
@code there is a notification showing successfully updated your project configuration
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code with the "VSCUSE_APP_EMEFY" workspace and Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit notifications visible.
Type 'Notifications: Clear All Notifications' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top of the window.
Press Enter to select "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" in the Visual Studio Code command palette.

Click the "Provision" button under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" sidebar of Visual Studio Code, identified by its person-and-gear icon and tooltip "Run the 'provision' lifecycle stage in m365agents.yml file."
Click on the "Enter API Key" input field in the pop-up dialog within Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents extension interface.
Type 'mysecretvalue' into the "Enter API Key in OpenAPI Description Document" input field in Visual Studio Code’s prompt dialog.
Press Enter to confirm the API key input in the "Enter API Key in OpenAPI Description Document" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Confirm" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit API key upload confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "4 New Notifications" icon in the bottom-right corner of the Visual Studio Code window to open recent alerts.
@assertion there's "provision stage executed successfully" exist.
